title: Uranium Structural Deficit Meets Hyperscaler Nuclear Pull
summary: Kazatomprom's 8M lb production cut, multi-year utility undercontracting, and accelerating hyperscaler nuclear PPAs have turned a cyclical uranium upcycle into a structural deficit through at least 2028, with Cameco (CCJ) the highest-quality Western pure-play producer.
catalyst: Spot uranium broke $100/lb in Q1 2026 (24% monthly gain to $101.26) before consolidating, the strongest move in years [1]. The structural driver is supply: Kazatomprom, the world's largest swing producer (~40% of mined output), lowered nominal 2026 capacity from 85M lbs to 77M lbs (8M lb cut) and confirmed in Q1 2026 it will NOT return to 100% production, judging current contract pricing inadequate to incentivize new capacity [2]. Cameco maintained its 2026 guidance of 19.5-21.5M lbs (their share) but flagged that multi-year delivery contracts now average over 28M lbs/year through 2030 — utilities are scrambling to secure pounds at any price [3].
thesis: Three demand forces are colliding with a constrained supply base. (1) Western utilities under-contracted aggressively from 2014-2022, leaving a ~10-15 year inventory replenishment cycle still in early innings; (2) hyperscaler nuclear PPAs — Microsoft/Constellation's Three Mile Island restart, Google/Kairos, Amazon/Talen — represent NEW uranium demand that didn't exist in pre-AI demand models; (3) policy alignment is unprecedented (US-Russia HALEU restrictions, Section 232 tariffs on enriched Russian uranium, accelerated DOE strategic stockpile builds). On the supply side, Kazatomprom's reset means the marginal incentive price for new pounds is structurally higher — likely $90-110/lb sustained vs. the $25-50/lb of the prior decade. Cameco (CCJ) captures this via long-term Western utility contracts AND a 49% stake in Westinghouse, the leading SMR/reactor services provider that benefits from the same nuclear demand wave on the equipment side.
counter_thesis: Three bear arguments deserve weight. (1) Spot prices have already moved — CCJ is up over 100% from 2023 lows and trades at 60x forward P/E, so much of the deficit thesis may be priced in; the real risk is multiple compression on any near-term spot pullback. (2) Kazatomprom's quotas are revocable — if spot uranium rallies further, they have explicit policy room to resume full production and crater prices. (3) Hyperscaler nuclear PPAs are real but are concentrated in restart and SMR timelines that may slip 2-3 years; the demand pull is back-end-loaded and 2027-2028 spot demand may disappoint vs. expectations. (4) CCJ's Westinghouse stake is illiquid, marked to fair value rather than market, and its accretion has been uneven quarter-to-quarter.

ticker_rationale.CCJ: Cameco is the only Western uranium pure-play of scale: Q1 2026 EPS of $0.34 beat by ~30%, adjusted EBITDA of $509M, and contract book averaging 28M+ lbs/year through 2030 [3]. The 49% Westinghouse stake adds reactor services optionality directly into the hyperscaler nuclear thesis. Forward P/E of 60.9x is high but EPS is depressed by Westinghouse mark-to-fair-value accounting; on through-cycle realized contract prices, the multiple compresses materially. Tier-1 jurisdictions (Canada, Australia, US) and a fortress balance sheet make CCJ the clean institutional vehicle for the structural deficit.

trade_construction.target_12mo: CCJ $150 (+35%) on spot uranium sustaining $90/lb+ and contract repricing flow-through
trade_construction.stop_loss: CCJ -15% from cost basis on closing basis, or thesis broken if Kazatomprom resumes full 2027 production
trade_construction.position_size_pct: 0.5-1.0% (On Our Radar sizing; relaxed cadence flag)

confidence_note: Filed under the 30-day cadence relaxation rule. Trade construction is qualitative — entry/target/stop are approximate based on current spot and historical contract economics, not a tight technical setup. What would promote to High Conviction: (a) confirmation of Kazatomprom holding the production cut in 2027 guidance (October 2026); (b) a fresh hyperscaler-utility uranium offtake disclosure; (c) Cameco Q3 2026 guidance lift on realized contract prices. Spot uranium pulling back below $85/lb without a corresponding hyperscaler PPA cancellation would NOT invalidate.

catalyst_calendar.event: Cameco Q2 2026 earnings — Westinghouse equity income and contract book commentary
Kazatomprom 2027 production guidance — extension of cut or resumption of full output
Cameco Q3 2026 earnings — realized uranium price and Westinghouse SMR pipeline update

risks: Kazatomprom reverses course and announces full 2027 production resumption — would compress spot uranium and CCJ multiple sharply
Hyperscaler SMR/restart timelines slip 2-3 years (likely), creating air-pocket in 2027 demand expectations
Westinghouse equity accounting can swing earnings $0.20-0.30/share quarter-to-quarter, creating headline volatility
60.9x forward P/E leaves no cushion for execution miss on contract pricing

invalidation.trigger: Kazatomprom announces 2027 production guidance >= 85M lbs (full production resumption)
Two consecutive quarters of spot uranium closing below $70/lb
Cameco lowers full-year production guidance below 18M lbs (their share) for 2026 or 2027
